reasons: Kalea's forced-position ultimate gives Lunox a cleaner target for fast magic burst.
Lunox can punish the landing point immediately when Kalea moves a high-value enemy into range.

proof.summary: Kalea grabs and moves a target into a predictable landing point, while Lunox follows with fast magic burst.
worksBestWhen: Kalea throws the target toward Lunox's range.
The target is a high-value damage dealer or fragile core.
failureCases: Lunox is too far from the landing point.
Kalea throws the target away from Lunox.
The target receives peel before Lunox finishes the burst.

reasons: Kalea can force the enemy toward Kadita's prepared burst path.
Kadita can punish Kalea's landing point with knock-up and burst damage.

proof.summary: Kalea moves the target into a chosen landing area, while Kadita punishes that area with knock-up and burst.
worksBestWhen: Kalea throws the target toward Kadita's prepared path.
Kadita waits for the landing point before committing burst.
failureCases: Kalea changes the landing direction unexpectedly.
Enemy crowd control interrupts Kadita before the burst lands.

reasons: Saber gives a second lock-on airborne burst after Kalea displaces the target.
Kalea can deliver a high-value target into Saber range for a simple pickoff sequence.

proof.summary: Kalea moves the target first, then Saber follows with single-target airborne burst.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits near Kalea's throw direction.
The target is a marksman, mage, or assassin.
failureCases: The target is too tanky or protected.

reasons: Eudora gives simple stun and burst follow-up after Kalea moves a target.
Kalea's displacement can put one enemy into Eudora's reliable combo range.

proof.summary: Kalea isolates a target, while Eudora follows with stun and burst damage.
worksBestWhen: Kalea throws the target into Eudora's range.
Eudora waits until the target lands before using full combo.
failureCases: Kalea throws the target outside Eudora's range.
Eudora's stun or burst is unavailable.
The target survives and receives peel.

reasons: Obsidia can follow Kalea's forced-position setup with Hunt of Bone.
Kalea's displacement gives Obsidia a clearer tether target and safer first attack window.

proof.summary: Kalea starts the forced-position pickoff, while Obsidia follows with tether and sustained ranged pressure.
worksBestWhen: Kalea throws the target into Obsidia's attack range.
Obsidia waits until Kalea confirms the target.
failureCases: Obsidia is too far to connect Hunt of Bone.
Obsidia is dived while chasing the tether.
The target is too tanky to finish during the window.
